ddls:
CREATE TABLE "network_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network request */,
  "source_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the source workload instance */,
  "source_namespace" TEXT /* Namespace of the source workload instance */,
  "source_principal" TEXT /* Identity of the source workload */,
  "request_header_key" TEXT /* Key of the HTTP request header */,
  "request_header_value" TEXT /* Value of the HTTP request header */,
  "auth_principal" TEXT /* Authenticated principal of the request */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the request */,
  "protocol" TEXT /* Protocol used for the request */,
  "is_tcp_supported" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the service supports TCP */,
  "request_method" TEXT /* HTTP method used for the request (e.g., GET, POST, PUT) */,
  "request_url" TEXT /* URL of the request */,
  "response_status" INTEGER /* HTTP status code of the response */,
  "response_body" TEXT /* Body of the HTTP response */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_requests_request_header_key FOREIGN KEY ("request_header_key") REFERENCES request_headers ("header_key"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_requests_auth_principal FOREIGN KEY ("auth_principal") REFERENCES auth_principals ("auth_principal_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_requests_source_principal FOREIGN KEY ("source_principal") REFERENCES principals ("principal_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_requests_source_ip FOREIGN KEY ("source_ip") REFERENCES ip_addresses ("ip_address"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_requests_source_namespace FOREIGN KEY ("source_namespace") REFERENCES namespaces ("namespace_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_requests_protocol FOREIGN KEY ("protocol") REFERENCES protocols ("protocol_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_requests_is_tcp_supported FOREIGN KEY ("is_tcp_supported") REFERENCES tcp_support ("is_tcp_supported")
)

CREATE TABLE "ip_addresses" (
  "ip_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each IP address */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address or CIDR range */,
  "is_cidr" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the IP is a CIDR range */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the IP address or range */,
  "geo_location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the IP address */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization associated with the IP address */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ip_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "namespaces" (
  "namespace_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each namespace */,
  "namespace_name" TEXT /* Name of the namespace */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the namespace */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the namespace was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the namespace was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("namespace_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "principals" (
  "principal_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each principal */,
  "principal_name" TEXT /* Name of the principal */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the principal */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the principal was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the principal was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("principal_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "request_headers" (
  "header_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request header */,
  "header_key" TEXT /* Key of the request header */,
  "header_value" TEXT /* Value of the request header */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the request header */,
  "sensitive" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the header value is sensitive */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("header_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "auth_principals" (
  "auth_principal_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each authenticated principal */,
  "auth_principal_name" TEXT /* Name of the authenticated principal */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the authenticated principal */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the authenticated principal was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the authenticated principal was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("auth_principal_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "protocols" (
  "protocol_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each protocol */,
  "protocol_name" TEXT /* Name of the protocol */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the protocol */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the protocol was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the protocol was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("protocol_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tcp_support" (
  "tcp_support_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each TCP support entry */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service */,
  "is_tcp_supported" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the service supports TCP */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of TCP support for the service */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the TCP support entry was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the TCP support entry was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tcp_support_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., security analyst, network admin, auditor) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network request being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the request data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the request data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_description" TEXT /* Description of the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES network_requests ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "security_incidents" (
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each security incident */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network request associated with the incident */,
  "incident_type" TEXT /* Type of the security incident (e.g., unauthorized access, suspicious activity) */,
  "incident_description" TEXT /* Description of the security incident */,
  "detected_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the incident was detected */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the incident (e.g., open, closed, under investigation) */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the incident (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_incidents_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES network_requests ("request_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident log entry */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the security incident */,
  "action_taken" TEXT /* Action taken during the incident */,
  "action_by" TEXT /* User who took the action */,
  "action_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the action was taken */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments or notes about the action */,
  "action_status" TEXT /* Status of the action (e.g., successful, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_logs_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES security_incidents ("incident_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_logs_action_by FOREIGN KEY ("action_by") REFERENCES users ("user_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_assignments" (
  "assignment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident assignment */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the security incident */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user assigned to investigate the incident */,
  "assigned_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the incident was assigned */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the assignment (e.g., open, in progress, closed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assignment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_assignments_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES security_incidents ("incident_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_assignments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "compliance_violations" (
  "violation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compliance violation */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network request associated with the violation */,
  "violation_type" TEXT /* Type of the compliance violation (e.g., GDPR, HIPAA) */,
  "violation_description" TEXT /* Description of the compliance violation */,
  "detected_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the violation was detected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("violation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compliance_violations_request_id FOREIGN KEY ("request_id") REFERENCES network_requests ("request_id")
)
